lesson.title: Factoring Trinomials (a ‚Č† 1)
lesson.body: For ax¬≤ + bx + c where a ‚Č† 1, use the **AC method**: (1) Find ac. (2) Find two numbers that multiply to ac and add to b. (3) Rewrite the middle term as two terms using those numbers. (4) Factor by grouping. Example: 2x¬≤ + 7x + 3. ac = 6. Pairs: {6,1} ‚Üí sum 7 ‚úď. Rewrite: 2x¬≤ + 6x + x + 3 = 2x(x+3) + 1(x+3) = (2x+1)(x+3).

rooms.question: For 3x¬≤ + 10x + 8, what is ac, and which pair of numbers multiplies to ac and sums to b = 10?
rooms.explanation: ac = 3√ó8 = 24. Find two numbers: product 24, sum 10. Test: 4√ó6=24 and 4+6=10 ‚úď. The pair {3,8} has sum 11, and {2,12} has sum 14.

rooms.question: Factor: 4x¬≤ ‚ąí 12x + 9
rooms.explanation: ac = 36, sum = ‚ąí12: {‚ąí6,‚ąí6}. Both numbers are equal ‚Üí perfect square trinomial: (2x‚ąí3)¬≤ = 4x¬≤‚ąí12x+9. Check: (2x)¬≤=4x¬≤, 2(2x)(‚ąí3)=‚ąí12x, (‚ąí3)¬≤=9 ‚úď. Recognizing perfect squares saves time.

rooms.question: Order the AC method steps to factor: 5x¬≤ + 13x + 6
items: Find pair with product 30 and sum 13: {3, 10}
rooms.explanation: AC method: compute ac ‚Üí find the splitting pair ‚Üí rewrite middle term ‚Üí group and factor. Check: (x+2)(5x+3) = 5x¬≤+3x+10x+6 = 5x¬≤+13x+6 ‚úď.

boss.name: The Leading Coefficient Daemon
boss.taunt: When a ‚Č† 1, neither do your chances.

questions.question: Factor completely: 12x¬≤ + 22x ‚ąí 20
questions.explanation: GCF = 2: 2(6x¬≤+11x‚ąí10). Now factor 6x¬≤+11x‚ąí10: ac=‚ąí60, sum=11: {15,‚ąí4}. Rewrite: 6x¬≤+15x‚ąí4x‚ąí10 = 3x(2x+5)‚ąí2(2x+5) = (3x‚ąí2)(2x+5). Final: 2(3x‚ąí2)(2x+5). Always check for a GCF first before applying the AC method.

questions.question: Factor: 3x¬≤ ‚ąí 11x + 6
questions.explanation: ac=18, sum=‚ąí11: {‚ąí9,‚ąí2}: (‚ąí9)+(‚ąí2)=‚ąí11 ‚úď. Rewrite: 3x¬≤‚ąí9x‚ąí2x+6 = 3x(x‚ąí3)‚ąí2(x‚ąí3) = (3x‚ąí2)(x‚ąí3). Check: (3x‚ąí2)(x‚ąí3)=3x¬≤‚ąí9x‚ąí2x+6=3x¬≤‚ąí11x+6 ‚úď. The (3x+2)(x‚ąí3) error uses +2 instead of ‚ąí2, giving 3x¬≤‚ąí7x‚ąí6.

questions.question: Solve: 6x¬≤ + x ‚ąí 2 = 0
questions.explanation: Factor: ac=‚ąí12, sum=1: {4,‚ąí3}. Rewrite: 6x¬≤+4x‚ąí3x‚ąí2 = 2x(3x+2)‚ąí1(3x+2) = (2x‚ąí1)(3x+2). Set each factor to 0: 2x‚ąí1=0 ‚Üí x=1/2; 3x+2=0 ‚Üí x=‚ąí2/3. Solutions: x=1/2 or x=‚ąí2/3. Check: 6(1/4)+(1/2)‚ąí2=1.5+0.5‚ąí2=0 ‚úď.
